title_en: Introduction: Ibn Sa'd and the Biographical Dictionary Tradition
content_en:
Kitab al-Tabaqat al-Kabir (The Great Book of Generations) by Muhammad ibn Sa'd (168–230 AH / 784–845 CE) is the foundational biographical dictionary of the Islamic tradition. Ibn Sa'd was the secretary (katib) of the great historian and hadith scholar al-Waqidi (130–207 AH), and his Tabaqat preserves not only his own research but also material gathered by al-Waqidi that would otherwise have been lost. The work provides biographies of the Prophet Muhammad, all of his Companions, and the major Muslim scholars of the first several generations — organized by 'generations' (tabaqat) in roughly chronological order.

The title reflects the organizing principle: a tabaqah (generation) in Islamic biographical literature refers to a group of people who lived and transmitted knowledge in roughly the same era and with comparable proximity to the Prophet. The Companions form the first tabaqah, the Successors (tabi'un) the second, and so on through the major scholars of the second and third Islamic centuries. This generational framework became the standard organizing principle for biographical dictionaries of hadith narrators.

The Significance of the Tabaqat Tradition

The Tabaqat al-Kabir is not merely a reference work but a reflection of the Islamic community's intense interest in the people through whom the Prophetic tradition was transmitted. To know the biography of a narrator — his or her tribe, teachers, students, character, travels, and the circumstances of transmission — was essential for evaluating the reliability of the hadiths associated with their name. The Tabaqat tradition therefore served the hadith critical tradition as its primary biographical source.

Ibn Sa'd's work is particularly valuable because it was compiled at a relatively early date (early third century AH), when many oral traditions about the Companions and Successors were still being transmitted by their students and grandstudents. Later biographical dictionaries had to rely on earlier written sources; Ibn Sa'd had access to living memory as well.

The Biography of the Prophet

The Tabaqat al-Kabir opens with a substantial biography of the Prophet Muhammad, drawing on the sirah tradition of Ibn Ishaq and al-Waqidi. This section covers the Prophet's lineage, birth, the years before prophethood, the first revelation, the Makkan and Madinan periods, the military campaigns, and the Prophet's death. Ibn Sa'd's biography is valuable for the early traditions it preserves, some of which do not appear in the later canonical sirah works.
title_id: Pengantar: Ibn Sa'd dan Tradisi Kamus Biografi
content_id:
Kitab al-Thabaqat al-Kabir (Kitab Besar tentang Generasi-generasi) karya Muhammad ibn Sa'd (168–230 H / 784–845 M) adalah kamus biografi foundational dari tradisi Islam. Ibn Sa'd adalah sekretaris (katib) dari sejarawan dan ulama hadis besar al-Waqidi (130–207 H), dan Thabaqat-nya melestarikan tidak hanya penelitiannya sendiri tetapi juga materi yang dikumpulkan oleh al-Waqidi yang sebaliknya mungkin telah hilang. Karya ini menyediakan biografi Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, semua Sahabatnya, dan ulama-ulama Muslim besar dari beberapa generasi pertama — diorganisir berdasarkan 'generasi-generasi' (thabaqat) dalam urutan kronologis yang kasar.

Judul karya mencerminkan prinsip pengorganisasiannya: sebuah thabaqah (generasi) dalam literatur biografi Islam merujuk pada sekelompok orang yang hidup dan mentransmisikan pengetahuan dalam era yang kira-kira sama dan dengan kedekatan yang sebanding dengan Nabi. Para Sahabat membentuk thabaqah pertama, para Tabi'in (Penerus) yang kedua, dan seterusnya melalui ulama-ulama besar abad ke-2 dan ke-3 Islam. Kerangka generasional ini menjadi prinsip pengorganisasian standar untuk kamus biografi perawi-perawi hadis.

Signifikansi Tradisi Thabaqat

Thabaqat al-Kabir bukan sekadar karya referensi tetapi cerminan dari minat intens komunitas Islam terhadap orang-orang melalui mana tradisi Kenabian ditransmisikan. Mengetahui biografi seorang perawi — suku atau kabilahnya, guru-gurunya, murid-muridnya, karakternya, perjalanannya, dan keadaan transmisi — sangat penting untuk mengevaluasi keandalan hadis-hadis yang dikaitkan dengan namanya. Tradisi Thabaqat dengan demikian melayani tradisi kritis hadis sebagai sumber biografi utamanya.

Karya Ibn Sa'd sangat berharga karena disusun pada tanggal yang relatif awal (awal abad ke-3 H), ketika banyak tradisi lisan tentang para Sahabat dan Tabi'in masih ditransmisikan oleh murid-murid dan cicit-cicit mereka. Kamus biografi kemudian harus mengandalkan sumber-sumber tertulis sebelumnya; Ibn Sa'd memiliki akses ke ingatan hidup juga.

Biografi Nabi ﷺ

Thabaqat al-Kabir dibuka dengan biografi substansial Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, bersandar pada tradisi sirah dari Ibn Ishaq dan al-Waqidi. Bagian ini mencakup nasab Nabi, kelahiran, tahun-tahun sebelum kenabian, wahyu pertama, periode Makkah dan Madinah, perang-perang, dan wafatnya Nabi. Biografi Ibn Sa'd sangat berharga untuk tradisi-tradisi awal yang dilestarikannya, beberapa di antaranya tidak muncul dalam karya-karya sirah kanonik kemudian.
content_ar: Kitab al-Tabaqat al-Kabir (The Great الكتاب of Generations) by محمد ﷺ ibn Sa'd (168–230 AH / 784–845 CE) is the foundational biographical dictionary of the Islamic التراث. Ibn Sa'd was the secretary ( katib ) of the great historian and الحديث scholar al-Waqidi (130–207 AH), and his Tabaqat preserves not only his own research but أيضاً material gathered by al-Waqidi that would otherwise have been lost. The work provides biographies of النبي ﷺ محمد ﷺ, all of his الصحابة, and the major مسلم العلماء of the أولاً several generations — organized by 'generations' ( tabaqat ) in roughly chronological order. The title reflects the organizing principle: a tabaqah (generation) in Islamic biographical literature refers to a group of people who lived and transmitted العلم in roughly the same era and with comparable proximity to النبي ﷺ. The الصحابة form the أولاً tabaqah, the التابعين ( tabi'un ) the ثانياً, and لذا on through the major العلماء of the ثانياً and ثالثاً Islamic centuries. This generational framework became the standard organizing principle for biographical dictionaries of الحديث narrators. The Significance of the Tabaqat التراث The Tabaqat al-Kabir is not merely a reference work but a reflection of the Islamic الجماعة's intense interest in the people through whom the Prophetic التراث was transmitted. لمعرفة the biography of a narrator — his or her tribe, teachers, students, الأخلاق, travels, and the circumstances of transmission — was الجوهري for evaluating the reliability of the hadiths associated with their name. The Tabaqat التراث لذلك served the الحديث البالغ الأهمية التراث as its الأساسي biographical source. Ibn Sa'd's work is particularly valuable لأن it was compiled at a relatively early date (early ثالثاً century AH), عندما many oral traditions about the الصحابة and التابعين were still being transmitted by their students and grandstudents. Later biographical dictionaries had to rely on earlier written sources; Ibn Sa'd had access to living memory as well. The Biography of النبي ﷺ The Tabaqat al-Kabir opens with a substantial biography of النبي ﷺ محمد ﷺ, drawing on the sirah التراث of Ibn Ishaq and al-Waqidi. This الفصل covers النبي ﷺ's lineage, birth, the years قبل prophethood, the أولاً الوحي, the Makkan and Madinan periods, the military campaigns, and النبي ﷺ's الموت. Ibn Sa'd's biography is valuable for the early traditions it preserves, some of which do not appear in the later canonical sirah works.
